title: [spec-compliance] LLSec byte mismatch in C tx vs spec example and Rust
description: C lichen_link_tx.c:100 computes llsec=(addr_mode & 0x03U) | (1U << 2) | 0x20U producing 0x25/0x26 while spec/09-packets-timing.md:48 example uses 0x21 and Rust link_layer.rs:486 + frame.rs produces 0x21/0x22. Breaks interop. Also C schnorr48.c:159-161,201-204 zero/low-order checks not strictly constant-time vs MUST in spec §5.3. From review of commits 7e57bdfbb,91b456fad touching lichen/subsys/lichen/link/schnorr48.c and link_layer.rs. See explore task ses_072d7b821ffedqrbywVW3J4DFq for details. --priority 1 --type bug
notes: Commit: ab09c1b7c LLSec fixed in tx.c + CT in schnorr48.c; interop now matches spec/Rust 0x2x bytes. Push conflicted due to bead history divergence (rebase aborted to avoid .beads merge issues)
close_reason: LLSec byte now produces 0x20/0x21/0x22 matching spec+Rust; Schnorr checks use crypto_verify32 for CT compliance. Verified via code review and vector alignment. Push to origin blocked by bead history divergence.
